text: Fill in the blanks on Larynx anatomy and clinical applications
questions: The *cricoid* cartilage is the only complete ring in the respiratory tract.
The larynx extends from vertebral level *C3* to C6.
The *posterior cricoarytenoid* is the only muscle that abducts the vocal cords, opening the rima glottidis.
The recurrent laryngeal nerve supplies all intrinsic muscles of the larynx except the *cricothyroid* muscle, which is supplied by the external laryngeal nerve.
The *cricothyroid* membrane (conus elasticus) is the site for emergency airway access (cricothyrotomy).
The interior of the larynx is divided into three compartments: vestibule, *ventricle*, and subglottis.
The free upper edge of the conus elasticus forms the *vocal* ligament.
Glottic carcinoma presents early with *hoarseness* because even small tumours affect vocal cord vibration.
The narrowest part of the adult larynx is the *rima glottidis*, while in children it is the subglottis at the cricoid ring.
The internal laryngeal nerve and superior laryngeal vessels pierce the *thyrohyoid* membrane to enter the larynx.
